CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jun-10-2015 Set For Trial On May-09-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Feb-10-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. KEVIN B. CLINE, ESQ. of Kerr & Wagstaffe, LLP, Tel No. 415-371-8500 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. CHAD D. GREESON, ESQ. of Archer Norris APLC, Tel No. 925-930-6600 appearing for the Defendant Daniel Fikes and Southwest Traders Inc.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANTS DANIEL FIKES AND SOUTHWEST TRADERS INC.'S AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION, Defendants' motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff's request for punitive damages is denied. Defendants' argument that punitive damage claims in the motor vehicle context are limited to intoxication cases lacks merit. (See Dawes v. Superior Court (1980) 111 Cal.App.3d 82, 90-91). There is a triable issue whether Mr. Fikes' U-turn was done with willful and conscious disregard of the safety of others. There is also a triable issue whether Michael Howard was a managing agent and whether he acted maliciously within the meaning of Civil Code 3294. Mr. Howard was in overall charge of the transportation department. (Clune Dec., Ex. F at 127:6-14.) He trained Mr. Fikes and told him that he could make the illegal U-Turn as part of servicing his route. (Clune Dec., Ex. B at 168:14-170:4.) A reasonable trier of fact could conclude by clear and convincing evidence that such conduct constitutes willful and conscious disregard of the safety of others. Defendant's stipulation to vicarious liability does not moot the claim for punitive damages under Diaz v. Carcamo (2011) 51 Cal.4th 1148, 1158. In Diaz the plaintiff did not seek punitive damages and is thus inapposite. Where, as here, a plaintiff seeks punitive damages based on the direct negligence of the employer, the analysis in Diaz is inapplicable. Defendants' evidentiary objections #1 and #2 are overruled. Defendants' evidentiary objections re: the Waldron declaration and the court records are sustained. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)
